text: this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语学习的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Pippa. And I'm Phil. What was your favourite game when you were a child, Pippa?
zh: 我是Pippa。我是Phil。Pippa,你小时候最喜欢玩什么游戏?

text: I think my favourite game was skipping with a skipping rope.
zh: 我想我最喜欢的游戏是跳绳。

text: How about you? Well, I could never skip, so I think I just preferred playing football.
zh: 你呢?我从来不会跳绳,所以我想我更喜欢踢足球。

text: Ah, well that's a good game to play in the playground. 80 years ago, it was quite common for children
zh: 啊,那是在操场上玩的好游戏。80年前,英国城市里的孩子

text: in Britain's cities to play in bomb sites, the ruins of houses which had been destroyed in World War II.
zh: 经常在废墟里玩耍,那些废墟是二战中被摧毁的房屋遗址。

text: Today the idea is back in fashion, with kids playing not in bomb sites,
zh: 如今这种玩法又重新流行起来,只不过孩子们玩的不是废墟,

text: but in junk playgrounds, also called adventure playgrounds.
zh: 而是垃圾游乐场,也叫冒险乐园。

text: In contrast to pre -made playgrounds where swings and slides are fixed in place,
zh: 与秋千、滑梯都固定好的预制游乐场不同,

text: adventure Adventure playgrounds provide pieces of building materials for kids to build things themselves.
zh: 冒险乐园提供各种建筑材料,让孩子们自己动手搭建东西。

text: Reporter William Cramer went to see one such playground in Wrexham, North Wales, for BBC World Service programme People Fixing the World.
zh: 记者William Cramer为BBC世界服务节目《解决世界难题的人们》,前往威尔士北部雷克瑟姆采访了这样一个游乐场。

text: At first glance, the land is little more than a junkyard.
zh: 乍一看,这片地方不过是个垃圾场。

text: There are stacks of used wooden pallets and big reels for holding wires.
zh: 那里堆着一摞摞用过的木托盘和缠电线用的大卷轴。

text: But if you look a little bit closer, you'll see crooked homemade structures, hidey holes and turrets.
zh: 但如果你仔细看,会发现歪歪扭扭的手工结构、藏身洞和小塔楼。

text: Children run barefoot, they swing on ropes and throw themselves down a makeshift water slide.
zh: 孩子们光着脚跑来跑去,荡绳子,还从简易水滑梯上滑下来。

text: At first glance William sees only junk. The phrase at first glance means looking at something for the first time
zh: William乍一看只看到垃圾。at first glance这个短语的意思是初次看某样东西

text: before having a chance to look more carefully. Adventure playgrounds are indeed full of junk,
zh: 还没来得及仔细观察。冒险乐园确实堆满了垃圾,

text: pieces of construction material like old tyres, wires, planks of wood and lengths of rope.
zh: 比如旧轮胎、电线、木板和绳子等建筑材料。

text: But look closely and you'll see children using the junk to play, building dens and hidey holes.
zh: 但仔细看,你会发现孩子们正用这些垃圾玩耍,搭建藏身处和秘密洞穴。

text: A hidey hole is a small place for hiding things, or in this case, for children to hide themselves.
zh: hidey hole指用来藏东西的小地方,在这里则是孩子们藏身的地方。

text: In this episode we'll be hearing how adventure playgrounds are giving kids the freedom and space to
zh: 在这一集里,我们将了解冒险乐园如何给孩子们自由和空间去

text: play. As usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases,
zh: 玩耍。像往常一样,我们会学习一些实用的新单词和短语,

text: and Remember, you can find all this episode's vocabulary along with a
zh: 记得,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本集的全部词汇和

text: transcript on our website, bbclearningenglish .com. But now I have a question for you,
zh: 文字稿。现在我有个问题要问你,

text: Pippa. We know that Adventure Playgrounds started off after the Second World War, but in which country?
zh: Pippa。我们知道冒险乐园是二战后开始出现的,但它起源于哪个国家?

text: Was it… a France, b Germany, or c Denmark?
zh: 是……a法国,b德国,还是c丹麦?

text: Hmm, I think maybe France. I think French children maybe were very adventurous.
zh: 嗯,我觉得可能是法国。我觉得法国孩子可能很有冒险精神。

text: OK, well we'll find out the answer to that question later in the programme.
zh: 好的,我们稍后在节目中揭晓答案。

text: Adventure playgrounds give kids the freedom to choose how they play.
zh: 冒险乐园让孩子们自由选择怎么玩。

text: According to child development expert Dr Anna Housley -Juster,
zh: 儿童发展专家Anna Housley-Juster博士认为,

text: this freedom is vital, as she explains here to BBC World Service's People Fixing the World.
zh: 这种自由至关重要,她在BBC世界服务《解决世界难题的人们》节目中做了解释。

text: It's true. We do tend to minimise play in certain ways,
zh: 确实如此。我们往往会在某些方面轻视玩耍,

text: but actually after the most most basic needs are met for children, so food, shelter, water, safe place to be.
zh: 但实际上,当孩子最基本的需求——食物、住所、水、安全的环境——得到满足后,

text: Play is by far the most important factor for healthy child development.
zh: 玩耍才是对儿童健康发展最重要的因素。

text: Anna thinks play is as important as a child's basic needs like food and safety.
zh: Anna认为,玩耍和食物、安全等基本需求同样重要。

text: But unfortunately, the importance of play tends to be minimised.
zh: 但不幸的是,玩耍的重要性常常被低估。

text: If something tends to happen, it happens often and is likely to happen again.
zh: 如果某事tend to发生,意味着它经常发生,而且很可能还会再次发生。

text: Anna says that after a child's basic needs are met, play is by far the most important factor in a healthy childhood.
zh: Anna说,当孩子的基本需求得到满足后,玩耍是健康童年中by far最重要的因素。

text: She uses the phrase by far to mean by a great amount.
zh: 她用by far这个短语表示“大大地、远远地”。

text: For example, your teacher might say you're by far the best student in
zh: 比如,老师可能会说你是班上by far最优秀的学生。

text: class. The freedom to take controlled risks in a safe environment is what Anna calls self -directed play.
zh: 在安全环境中承担可控风险的自由,Anna称之为“自主玩耍”。

text: Here she explains more to Myra Anubi, presenter of BBC programme People Fixing the the world.
zh: 她向BBC节目《解决世界难题的人们》主持人Myra Anubi做了进一步解释。

text: So what you're saying is letting kids play free play
zh: 所以你的意思是,让孩子们自由玩耍,

text: actually or play on their own and kind of come up with the ideas that's really crucial for their development.
zh: 或者自己玩、自己想点子,这对他们的发展真的至关重要。

text: It's 100 % crucial for their development in what I meant when I said that
zh: 这对他们的发展100%至关重要,我刚才说它有“保护作用”,意思是

text: it's protective is that kids build resiliency, confidence, coping strategies, regulation strategies.
zh: 孩子们能借此培养出韧性、自信、应对策略和自我调节能力。

text: So many of the skills that we know lead to healthy human development are developed in play.
zh: 我们知道很多有益于健康人格发展的技能,都是在玩耍中培养出来的。

text: Myra mentions the importance of free play and Anna says it's 100 % crucial, meaning completely.
zh: Myra提到自由玩耍的重要性,Anna说这100%至关重要,意思是“完全”。

text: You can also say 100 % as an informal response to mean yes, I agree with you completely.
zh: 你也可以用100%作为非正式的回应,表示“是的,我完全同意”。

text: Adventure playgrounds have lots of small dangers, hard pieces of wood, sharp metal, nails.
zh: 冒险乐园里有很多小风险,比如硬木块、锋利的金属、钉子。

text: According to Anna, taking small risks when playing with these makes kids more resilient and builds
zh: Anna认为,玩这些东西时承担小风险,会让孩子更有韧性,并培养出

text: coping strategies, a term for the psychological ways children develop to manage challenging situations.
zh: 应对策略——这个词指的是孩子发展出的、用来应对困难情境的心理方法。

text: And Phil, I think it's time for the answer to your quiz question.
zh: Phil,我想是时候公布你那个测验问题的答案了。

text: Yes, I asked you in which country Adventure Playgrounds started.
zh: 是的,我问的是冒险乐园最早出现在哪个国家。

text: You said France, but I'm afraid the correct answer was Denmark.
zh: 你说是法国,但恐怕正确答案是丹麦。

text: They were introduced by Danish architect Carl Theodor Sorensen.
zh: 它们是由丹麦建筑师Carl Theodor Sorensen引入的。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with the phrase at first glance,
zh: 好,我们来复习一下今天学到的词汇,先从at first glance这个短语开始,

text: where you see something quickly without a chance to consider it more carefully.
zh: 意思是匆匆一看某样东西,还没来得及仔细思考。

text: A hidey -hole is a small place for hiding things in, or hiding yourself in.
zh: hidey-hole指用来藏东西的小地方,也可以是自己藏身的地方。

text: If people tend to do something, they do it often and will probably continue to do it in the future.
zh: 如果人们tend to做某事,意味着他们经常这样做,而且以后可能还会继续。

text: The phrase by far means by a great amount.
zh: by far这个短语的意思是“大大地”。

text: Saying 100 % means completely and can be used to emphasise a statement or show that you totally agree with it.
zh: 说100%意味着“完全”,可以用来强调一句话,或表示你完全同意。

text: And finally, coping strategies are psychological ways of managing your emotions in challenging situations.
zh: 最后,coping strategies指的是在困难情境中管理自己情绪的心理方法。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but remember, you can find many more trending topics,
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了,不过别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到更多热门话题,

text: plus a quiz and worksheet for this episode on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 以及本集的测验和练习题。

text: Hope to see you there soon, but for now, it's Goodbye.
zh: 希望很快在那里见到你,不过现在,再见啦。
